summary: Iteration 2 testing for Refixion complete. Backend: 37/37 pytest passing (14 new iteration-2 tests for /site-content, /admin/site-content, /seo, /admin/seo, /admin/brands, /admin/devices CRUD, /admin/price-overrides lifecycle, email defaults, plus all 23 iteration-1 regressions). Frontend: /admin/devices price override modal persists value and restores default on clear (verified via UI + backend); /admin/content saves hero.headline_line1 and change is reflected on homepage after invalidateSiteContent; /admin/seo update to '/' title propagates to document.title on homepage; homepage renders all dynamic sections (hero, trust, how_it_works, brands, why, reviews, faq, cta, footer). No regressions on existing admin pages (bookings, repair-methods, workshop, email settings at /admin/email).

issues: Escape key does not close the modal; only the 'Klaar' button and clicking backdrop close it. Consider a keydown Escape handler for consistency.

action_items: (Optional UX) Add ESC-key close handler to the DevicePricesModal in AdminDevicesPage.jsx.

(Still open from iter-1) Update <title> in /app/frontend/public/index.html — but note it is now dynamically overwritten by useSeo.js on route change, so initial-load flash may still show 'Emergent | Fullstack App'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from ~700 to ~789 lines — split into routers (admin/public/content) before it gets bigger.

PUT /api/admin/site-content whitelists top-level sections but does not validate nested shape — a malformed payload from a compromised admin token could break the homepage. Consider Pydantic models per section for defensive validation.

PUT /api/admin/seo does not validate that 'path' starts with '/' or is one of the known SEO paths — anyone with admin can create arbitrary SEO documents. Low risk (admin-only) but tighten with an allowlist.
get_site_content returns {} when the seeded doc is missing — HomePage should guard for missing sections (it already uses useSiteContent hook — verify defensive access).
In AdminContentPage.jsx the save button posts ALL sections every time even if only one changed — fine for now but inefficient; consider dirty-tracking.
AdminDevicesPage price modal: pressing Escape does not close (no keydown listener). Backdrop click works because outer div has onClick={onClose}.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (devices price override, content editor, SEO editor, homepage dynamic content, admin regressions)

seed_data_creation: None persisted — all mutations restored: hero.headline_line1 restored, /pricing SEO title restored, dev-ip11 popular flag restored, dev-ip16pm rep-screen override deleted, created TEST device deleted, / SEO title restored, Samsung device price override cleared.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests: pytest.ini pins -n 2 --dist loadscope. To run serial: pytest -n 0 /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Admin email settings page lives at /admin/email (NOT /admin/email-settings — that returns 404 which is expected). Device price modal closes via 'Klaar' button or backdrop click, NOT Escape key. Playwright must use page.get_by_role('button', name='Klaar').click() to close before reopening.
